intro:
There is no information about the plant Peramium decipiens, so I will provide general information based on the closest match, Peramium decipiens appears to be an alternative name for Peruvian mint, also known as Peramium or Mentha citrata var. perennis.

Peramium decipiens, also known as Peruvian mint, is a plant species within the mint family (Lamiaceae). The leaves and essential oil of Peruvian mint have been used in traditional medicine to alleviate various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its carminative, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

The bioactive constituents of Peruvian mint include menthol, menthone, limonene, and β-caryophyllene, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. These compounds have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to the plant's medicinal properties.

Peruvian mint is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used to treat various health conditions. Herbal preparations made with Peruvian mint include teas, tinctures, and essential oil extracts, which are often used to alleviate digestive issues, relieve headaches, and promote relaxation.
